channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Putin visits China to reaffirm Russia ties
item.description: The Kremlin has said Putin and Xi plan to discuss economic cooperation between the two countries, but also "key international and regional issues."
item.content:encoded:
The Kremlin has said Putin and Xi plan to discuss economic cooperation between the two countries, but also "key international and regional issues."

(Image credit: Alexander Kazakov)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says he's called off Iran strike at request of Gulf allies
item.description: President Donald Trump says he is holding off on a military strike on Iran planned for Tuesday because "serious negotiations" are underway to end the war.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump says he is holding off on a military strike on Iran planned for Tuesday because "serious negotiations" are underway to end the war.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses primary. And, WHO declares Ebola outbreak a global emergency
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.

(Image credit: Tyler Kaufman)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
item.description: The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.
item.content:encoded:
The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.

(Image credit: Charles Sykes)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ukraine launches large-scale drone strikes on Russia
item.description: As peace talks stall, Ukraine launches one of the biggest drone strikes against Russia since the beginning of Russia's full-scale invasion in an attempt to weaken Russia's war machine.
item.content:encoded: As peace talks stall, Ukraine launches one of the biggest drone strikes against Russia since the beginning of Russia's full-scale invasion in an attempt to weaken Russia's war machine.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: North Korea is relieved its nuclear weapons have prevented a fate like Iran's
item.description: The U.S. long pushed for both Iran and North Korea to quit their nuclear plans. After seeing Iran's leader get killed, North Korea's Kim Jong Un believes he made the right choice to stay the course.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. long pushed for both Iran and North Korea to quit their nuclear plans. After seeing Iran's leader get killed, North Korea's Kim Jong Un believes he made the right choice to stay the course.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: U.S. and Israel prepare for potential return to full-scale war with Iran
item.description: Events over the weekend highlight the risk of renewed war, as the United States and Israel signal they would be ready to fight Iran again.
item.content:encoded: Events over the weekend highlight the risk of renewed war, as the United States and Israel signal they would be ready to fight Iran again.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Peru's electoral board confirms June 7 presidential runoff
item.description: Authorities confirmed on Sunday the official results of the first round of the presidential elections in early April, with Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez advancing to the runoff on June 7.
item.content:encoded:
Authorities confirmed on Sunday the official results of the first round of the presidential elections in early April, with Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez advancing to the runoff on June 7.

(Image credit: Guadalupe Pardo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Drone strikes UAE nuclear plant highlighting risk of renewed war
item.description: A drone strike sparked a fire on the edge of the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant on Sunday in what authorities called an "unprovoked terrorist attack."
item.content:encoded:
A drone strike sparked a fire on the edge of the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant on Sunday in what authorities called an "unprovoked terrorist attack."

(Image credit: Arun Girija/Emirates News Agency)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Executions nearly doubled in the U.S. last year, and soared abroad
item.description: The number of people killed in state-sanctioned executions worldwide rose to a 44-year high in 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International.
item.content:encoded:
The number of people killed in state-sanctioned executions worldwide rose to a 44-year high in 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International.

(Image credit: Curt Anderson)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise
